decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing Underworld Breach along with powerful cards like Brain Freeze, Tinker, and Yawgmoth's Will to generate infinite combos and win the game quickly. It includes fast mana acceleration like Mana Crypt, Mana Vault, and Moxen to help cast big spells early. The deck also plays disruption spells like Force of Will and Thoughtseize to protect its game plan from opponents. With the ability to recur spells from the graveyard and draw cards with Dack Fayden and Dig Through Time, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces and take over the game swiftly.

The deck aims to control the game through cards like Show and Tell, Stock Up, Force of Will, and Force of Negation. The key strategy revolves around using Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures into play, such as Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Oko, Thief of Crowns to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to manipulate the board state and outvalue the opponent to ultimately secure victory.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining card advantage and tempo. The deck includes a combination of powerful countermagic spells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce, along with efficient removal spells like Fatal Push. The inclusion of cards like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time allows the player to draw into key pieces of their strategy, while cards like Demonic Tutor and Merchant Scroll provide additional flexibility. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Null Rod and Undercity Sewers to further control the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and card draw to generate a large amount of mana and cards while controlling the opponent's game plan. By casting spells like Paradoxical Outcome and Stock Up, the player can draw multiple cards and replay powerful artifacts like Moxen for additional mana to continue their combo. The inclusion of Force of Will and other counterspells helps protect the player's resources and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to assemble a powerful board state quickly and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells and artifacts.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using disruptive spells like Force of Will and Thoughtseize to control the game while also utilizing powerful card draw with Ancestral Recall and Treasure Cruise. The deck has a strong emphasis on tempo plays with cards like Time Walk and Spell Pierce, as well as efficient removal like Fatal Push. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value by recurring important creatures and spells from the graveyard. The deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents through careful resource management and strategic decision-making.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 Esper Control deck focuses on manipulating the opponent's resources while generating card advantage and controlling the game through counterspells and removal spells. The deck utilizes powerful mana acceleration with Moxen and fast mana sources like Sol Ring and Black Lotus to cast game-changing spells like Ancestral Recall, Time Walk, and Tinker. Additionally, the deck includes versatile cards like Bolas's Citadel and Yawgmoth's Will to generate value and create powerful board states. The deck's main goal is to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent through efficient control and resource management.

The deck focuses on using powerful artifacts and mana acceleration to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats. It utilizes cards like Mishra's Workshop, Mana Vault, and Ancient Tomb to play out threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Scrawling Crawler ahead of schedule. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator and Time Vault provide additional utility and win conditions. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle while applying consistent pressure with efficient threats and mana acceleration.

The deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive artifact creatures and disruptive artifacts. It uses Mishra's Workshop to accelerate its mana production and plays cards like Sphere of Resistance and Trinisphere to hinder the opponent's game plan. It also utilizes cards like Nettlecyst and Stonecoil Serpent to apply pressure and close out the game quickly. With the ability to disrupt the opponent's strategies while presenting a fast clock, this deck aims to win the game before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on deploying disruptive hatebear creatures and stax pieces to slow down the opponent's game plan while maintaining a fast aggressive strategy. It utilizes cards like Archon of Emeria, Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, and Void Mirror to disrupt the opponent's spells and resources, while also deploying quick beatdown creatures like White Plume Adventurer and Clarion Conqueror to apply pressure. The deck also has some powerful mana acceleration with cards like Moxen and Ancient Tomb to quickly deploy its threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through disruption and removal spells like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Spell Pierce. It also utilizes powerful control cards like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade and Force of Negation to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes efficient card draw with Ancestral Recall, Brainstorm, and Dig Through Time to ensure a steady flow of resources. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Psychic Frog and Orcish Bowmasters provide utility and additional disruption options. The deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents through careful resource management and strategic plays.

This deck focuses on disrupting opponents with hatebears like Archon of Emeria and Chancellor of the Annex, while also applying pressure with aggro creatures like Clarion Conqueror and Witch Enchanter. It also utilises disruptive lands like Wasteland and Karakas to hinder opponents' game plan. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Black Lotus and Moxen allows for explosive starts, while cards like Chalice of the Void and Void Mirror provide additional disruption. The overall strategy is to control the game through hate effects while still posing a fast clock with aggressive creatures.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting opponents' strategies with counterspells, removal, and hand disruption while generating card advantage through spells like Ancestral Recall and Treasure Cruise. It also utilizes powerful artifacts like Black Lotus and Null Rod to control the game's tempo. The deck aims to control the board and outvalue opponents with efficient threats like Snapcaster Mage and Lurrus of the Dream-Den, while utilizing synergies between cards like Psychic Frog and Orcish Bowmasters to maintain control over the game.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt and slow down the opponent through the use of hatebears and stax effects, while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures. By combining disruptive elements like Chancellor of the Annex, Archon of Emeria, and Void Mirror with aggressive threats like White Plume Adventurer and Clarion Conqueror, this deck aims to control the board and limit the opponent's options. The inclusion of cards like Anointed Peacekeeper and Seasoned Dungeoneer provide additional utility and card advantage to stay ahead in the game. Additionally, the mana acceleration from cards like Ancient Tomb and Chrome Mox allows for a fast start to apply pressure early on.

The deck focuses on utilizing the combo potential of Underworld Breach to quickly and efficiently recur powerful spells from the graveyard. The goal is to create a loop where spells can be continuously cast from the graveyard while generating immense value and overwhelming the opponent. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds additional recursion potential and flexibility to the deck's strategy. The deck also features a mix of counterspells, card draw, and mana acceleration to support the combo and protect it from disruption.

The main idea of this deck is to assemble the Coveted Jewel combo as quickly as possible, using cards like Mishra's Workshop, Mana Crypt, and Grim Monolith to ramp into it. Once the Coveted Jewel is in play, the deck aims to generate a big advantage by drawing cards and gaining extra resources. Cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Mystic Forge help to keep the combo going and find key pieces. Additionally, the deck includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Trinisphere to protect the combo and hinder the opponent's game plan.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly deploy disruptive creatures that hinder the opponent's game plan while also being aggressive with cards like Clarion Conqueror and White Plume Adventurer. The deck utilizes hate cards like Archon of Emeria and Void Mirror to disrupt the opponent's spells, while also using utility lands like Wasteland and Karakas to further disrupt their mana base. The deck also includes mana acceleration and card draw to ensure a consistent and powerful early game presence.
